context.project_description: Add LLM token usage tracking to monitor query costs across OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, and Ollama providers by instrumenting LLM calls and exposing usage data via Answer metadata.
key_requirements: Track input/output tokens separately for chat and embedding operations
Expose token usage data through Answer metadata in SDK responses
Work with existing LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider) without API changes
Integrate with existing instrumentation system for hook-based tracking
constraints: Must work with current LLM provider protocols (ChatProvider, EmbeddingProvider)
Cannot change public API interfaces significantly
Should leverage existing instrumentation system in fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py
All provider types (chat, embed) must be covered consistently
context.existing_context: fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py, fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py, fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py, fitz_ai/core/answer.py
stakeholders: Developers who need insight into which queries are most expensive
Product Teams who require data for budgeting and pricing model decisions
Operations team who wants monitoring of LLM spend across services

in_scope: Add instrumentation hooks for LLM calls
Track input/output tokens in provider implementations
Expose usage data via Answer metadata (for SDK users)
out_of_scope: Real-time cost calculation with pricing tiers

existing_files: fitz_ai/core/instrumentation.py — Instrumentation system for benchmarking plugin performance
fitz_ai/llm/providers/base.py — Provider protocols for LLM clients
fitz_ai/llm/providers/openai.py — OpenAI provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/anthropic.py — Anthropic provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/cohere.py — Cohere provider wrappers using the official SDK
fitz_ai/llm/providers/ollama.py — Ollama provider wrappers using direct HTTP calls
needed_artifacts: fitz_ai/llm/instrumentation/token_tracking.py — Core tracking logic and hook implementation

assumptions.impact: Adding complexity if not well-integrated, potentially breaking existing benchmarking functionality
API compatibility issues if users don't expect this data in SDK responses

assumptions.assumption: Token Counting Implementation Varies by Provider
assumptions.impact: Inaccurate counts could mislead users if not properly marked, especially for Ollama/local models requiring heuristic estimation
Missed intermediate tracking without special handling could result in incomplete token usage data for streaming responses
Users may not be satisfied with metadata-only exposure unless prominently documented, potentially requiring additional API mechanisms later

assumptions.assumption: Users Want Detailed Breakdown by Provider Type
assumptions.impact: May require more complex reporting structures if users prefer provider-specific metrics over unified totals

approaches.name: Instrumentation-Based Tracking
approaches.description: Extend the existing instrumentation system to capture token usage metadata from LLM provider calls using BenchmarkHook interface modifications.
pros: Leverages existing infrastructure without major architectural changes
Minimal impact on core logic and interfaces
Provides consistent tracking across all LLM providers
cons: Requires modifying 5+ LLM provider implementations to return structured results with usage data
Instrumentation system needs enhancement to handle metadata aggregation
May introduce slight overhead in method call wrapping
best_for: Projects wanting minimal disruption to existing codebase structure

approaches.name: Direct Provider Modification Approach
approaches.description: Modify all LLM provider interfaces and implementations to return structured results including token usage data, then aggregate at the engine level.
pros: Clean separation of concerns with explicit token tracking in each provider
Easier debugging and direct access to per-call usage information
Clear API contract for token consumption
cons: Requires extensive changes across multiple files (5+ LLM providers)
Breaking change if not done carefully with backward compatibility layers
More complex implementation due to interface modifications
best_for: Projects needing robust token usage tracking and full visibility into consumption per method call

architecture.reasoning: The Instrumentation-Based Tracking approach is recommended because it leverages the existing, well-established instrumentation framework which already handles benchmarking and tracing. This approach minimizes disruption to core components while extending functionality through hooks rather than rewriting interfaces. It follows the pattern of using existing mechanisms (InstrumentedProxy, BenchmarkHook) for cross-cutting concerns like telemetry. The second approach would require extensive interface changes across 5+ provider files and risk breaking backward compatibility. Since the codebase already has Instrumentation system in place with hooks for timing/calls, extending it to capture token usage metadata is more maintainable than modifying all LLM providers' return types.

key_tradeoffs.backward_compatibility_vs_feature_enhanc: Adding optional token_usage fields maintains backward compatibility but requires careful implementation to avoid breaking changes in existing APIs
key_tradeoffs.instrumentation_complexity_vs_tracking_a: Extending the hook system to capture metadata adds complexity but ensures accurate aggregation of usage across method calls
key_tradeoffs.modular_extension_vs_system_overhaul: Leveraging existing instrumentation framework avoids rebuilding from scratch while maintaining consistency with established patterns

technology_considerations: Python dataclasses for structured Answer model with optional token_usage field
TypedDict for standardized result types (ChatResult, EmbeddingResult) to ensure consistent metadata handling across LLM providers
Instrumentation system extension using BenchmarkHook interface for capturing usage metadata without disrupting existing timing/call tracking
Protocol-based interfaces in LLM provider implementations to enforce structured return types while maintaining flexibility
Optional fields with None defaults in API response models (QueryResponse, ChatResponse) to preserve backward compatibility
architecture.scope_statement: Extend existing instrumentation framework to capture and aggregate LLM token consumption across chat, embed, rerank, and vision operations. Requires modifications to Answer class, provider interfaces, and API response models while maintaining backward compatibility.

adrs.title: ADR: Token Usage Tracking in LLM Calls
adrs.context: The system needs to track and expose token usage from all LLM API calls (chat, embed, rerank, vision) so users can understand the cost of each query. This requires modifications across multiple components to capture, aggregate, and expose this information.
adrs.decision: Extend Answer class with optional token_usage field, update provider interfaces to return structured results including usage data, enhance instrumentation system to capture metadata, modify API response models, and update SDK methods to propagate usage back to callers.
adrs.rationale: This approach leverages existing architectural patterns (dataclasses, protocols, instrumentation) while maintaining backward compatibility through optional fields. It follows the established codebase conventions for extending functionality without breaking changes.
consequences: All LLM provider implementations must be updated to return structured results with usage data
Instrumentation system requires extension to capture and aggregate token usage metadata
API response models need modification to include token_usage in responses
SDK interfaces will need updates to propagate usage information back to clients
Answer class changes are backward compatible due to optional field
alternatives_considered: Create separate tracking service instead of extending Answer - rejected because it would require additional coordination and break existing patterns
Modify provider methods to return tuples with usage data - rejected because it breaks established protocols and is less maintainable than structured results
Add global token counter without method-level granularity - rejected because it doesn't provide detailed cost visibility needed by users

adrs.title: ADR: Centralized Token Usage Tracking vs. Distributed Metric Collection
adrs.context: Instrumentation-Based Tracking relies on a central TokenUsageHook to aggregate token usage across all operations, whereas an alternative approach could distribute token counting to individual components that know their own usage patterns.
adrs.decision: Use centralized TokenUsageHook in InstrumentedProxy rather than distributed token tracking within each component
adrs.rationale: This decision maintains consistency with existing instrumentation patterns and reduces the risk of double-counting or missed measurements. The central hook ensures all operations are tracked uniformly without requiring changes to individual components.
consequences: Single point of failure for token tracking - if TokenUsageHook fails, token data may be lost
Increased coupling between core components (FitzKragEngine) and instrumentation layer (InstrumentedProxy)
Potential performance bottleneck during high-throughput operations due to centralized processing
alternatives_considered: Distributed approach where each component maintains its own token usage counters and reports them via existing telemetry hooks
Hybrid model that uses distributed counting but central aggregation for final reporting

components.name: Answer
components.purpose: Paradigm-agnostic answer representation with optional token usage tracking
responsibilities: Store text response from LLM queries
Maintain provenance information for source attribution
Optionally track token consumption across LLM operations

components.name: FitzKragEngine
components.purpose: Knowledge Routing Augmented Generation engine that answers queries with token usage tracking
responsibilities: Load collections and initialize components
Execute query processing pipeline including LLM calls
Aggregate token usage data from all operations in the pipeline
Return Answer objects containing both results and usage metrics
interfaces: _store_cloud_cache(query_text: str, addresses: list, answer: Answer) -> None

components.name: InstrumentedProxy
components.purpose: Wrapper that adds instrumentation to track method calls including token usage metadata
responsibilities: Wrap LLM provider objects with timing and usage tracking hooks
Capture call start/end events for benchmarking purposes
Collect and aggregate token consumption data from wrapped methods
interfaces: __init__(target: Any, layer: str, plugin_name: str, methods_to_track: set[str] | None)

components.name: TokenUsageHook
components.purpose: Instrumentation hook that captures and aggregates token usage from LLM calls
responsibilities: Track method call start events with context information
Capture result metadata including token usage when available
Aggregate usage data by method name for reporting
interfaces: on_call_start(layer: str, plugin_name: str, method: str, args: tuple, kwargs: dict) -> Any
on_call_end(context: Any, result: Any, error: Exception | None) -> None

Return Answer objects that may include token usage information

ask(question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer

integration_points: ExternalSystem — OpenAI API calls for chat, embedding, and vision services
ExternalSystem — Anthropic API calls for chat and vision services
ExternalSystem — Cohere API calls for chat, embedding, and reranking services
ExternalSystem — Ollama API calls for chat, embedding, reranking, and vision services

artifacts.content:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List, Optional

from fitz_ai.core.answer_mode import AnswerMode
from fitz_ai.core.provenance import Provenance

def _validate_answer_mode(mode: AnswerMode) -> None:
    # Remove the `defined` check since it's not used and always True
    pass

@dataclass
class Answer:
    text: str
    provenance: List[Provenance]
    constraints: Optional[List[str]] = None
    token_usage: Optional[dict[str, int]] = None

    def __post_init__(self):
        _validate_answer_mode(AnswerMode.TEXT)
artifacts.purpose: Extends the Answer dataclass to include optional token_usage field for tracking LLM token consumption
Updates LLM provider protocols to return structured results with token usage metadata instead of simple types
Enhances the instrumentation system with a TokenUsageHook to capture and aggregate token usage metadata from LLM calls
Modifies FitzKragEngine.answer() to accumulate and return token usage data in Answer objects

        # ... existing init logic ...

    def ask(self, question: str, top_k: Optional[int], conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer:
        # ... existing logic ...
        
        engine_result = self.engine.answer(Query(text=question, context=conversation_context))
        return engine_result  # Already includes token_usage after changes
    
    def query(self, question: str, conversation_context: Optional['ConversationContext']) -> Answer:
        # ... existing logic ...
        
        engine_result = self.engine.answer(Query(text=question, context=conversation_context))
        return engine_result  # Already includes token_usage after changes
    # ... rest of class unchanged ...

phases.name: Read Before Writing
phases.objective: Verify integration points by reading source
phases.verification_command: pytest tests/llm/providers/test_base.py tests/llm/providers/test_openai.py tests/llm/providers/test_anthropic.py tests/llm/providers/test_cohere.py tests/llm/providers/test_ollama.py -v

phases.name: Create TokenUsage Data Model
phases.objective: Implement a data model to represent token usage information for LLM operations
deliverables: New file fitz_ai/core/token_usage.py with TokenUsage dataclass
key_risks: Data model may not be extensible enough for all provider formats
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage; t = TokenUsage(100, 200); print(t.to_dict())"

phases.name: Implement Instrumentation Hook for Token Tracking
phases.objective: Create a hook that captures token usage metadata during LLM calls through the existing instrumentation system
deliverables: New file fitz_ai/core/token_tracking.py with TokenUsageHook class and utility functions
key_risks: Provider response formats vary significantly across LLM providers
Hook may not correctly extract token usage from all provider responses
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.token_tracking import TokenUsageHook; h = TokenUsageHook(); print('Hook created successfully')"

phases.name: Modify Answer Class to Support Token Usage
phases.objective: Extend the Answer class to handle token usage data in its metadata field
key_risks: Metadata aggregation logic may not handle edge cases properly
phases.verification_command: python -c "from fitz_ai.core.answer import Answer; from fitz_ai.core.token_usage import TokenUsage; a = Answer('test'); t = TokenUsage(10, 20); a.add_token_usage(t); print(a.metadata['token_usage'])"

phases.name: Update SDK Interface to Attach Token Usage
phases.objective: Modify the SDK's ask/query methods to consume token usage data from hooks and attach it to answers
deliverables: Updated fitz_ai/sdk/fitz.py with token usage attachment logic
key_risks: Integration may not work properly if hooks aren't registered correctly
Performance impact from hook processing in hot path

risks.description: Token tracking hook may not correctly extract token usage from all provider response formats
risks.mitigation: Implement comprehensive fallback strategies in _extract_tokens_from_response to handle various LLM provider response structures
risks.contingency: Log extraction failures and fall back to zero token tracking with warning messages
risks.verification: Test with OpenAI, Cohere, Anthropic responses using mock data

risks.description: Instrumentation hooks may impact performance due to metadata collection overhead during LLM calls
risks.mitigation: Use lazy initialization and conditional hook registration based on feature flags
risks.contingency: Implement optional token tracking via configuration flag with default disabled
risks.verification: Benchmark execution time differences when hooks enabled vs disabled

risks.description: Token aggregation logic may not properly handle multiple LLM calls in a single query flow
risks.mitigation: Implement proper token key generation using layer/plugin/method combinations to distinguish different LLM operations
risks.contingency: Add unit tests for multi-call scenarios and verify correct aggregation via test fixtures
risks.verification: Write integration tests covering multiple LLM calls within a single ask() invocation

risks.description: Some LLM providers might not expose token usage in response objects causing silent failures
risks.mitigation: Implement robust error handling and graceful degradation with empty tokens when extraction fails
risks.contingency: Create fallback mechanism to estimate token counts based on text length if actual token data unavailable
risks.verification: Verify all major provider types (OpenAI/Cohere/Anthropic/Ollama) handle gracefully

recommended_contingencies: Add configuration flag to disable token tracking when performance is critical
Implement fallback estimation logic for token counts based on text length if provider doesn't supply usage data
Create comprehensive unit and integration tests covering all supported LLM providers' response formats
